$comment_purpose: G1N W9 — Cloudflare CDN configuration. Two surfaces: Pages (client) + Workers (API gateway). This JSON is the deploy-time source of truth; the wrangler / pages.toml files are derived from it. NEVER cache LLM output.

account.$comment_account: Deploy-time override via the CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID env var; do NOT commit a real account id.

pages.$comment_pages: Cloudflare Pages hosts the Electron renderer + Vite-built static client. The output directory is client/dist/; build command is `npm run build` (vite build). Direct Upload mode (no git integration) keeps the build artefact deterministic.

deploymentTrigger.$comment_deploymentTrigger: CI uploads the built dist/ via `wrangler pages deploy` after the Vite build; pages' git integration is intentionally NOT used (the build needs the case_01 scene YAML copied into the bundle).

environmentVariables.$comment_environmentVariables: VITE_API_BASE points at the Workers API gateway. VITE_CDN_BASE points at the R2-backed static asset CDN (separate bucket; see r2.buckets[]).

headers.$comment_headers: Pages-level headers apply to every static asset. The per-type overrides live in cache.rules[]; this block is the unconditional layer (security headers).

redirects.$comment_redirects: The Pages site proxies /api/* to the Workers API gateway. This keeps the client a single-origin app (no CORS preflight on the hot path).

workers.$comment_workers: Cloudflare Workers is the API gateway in front of the FastAPI origin (k8s). It does TLS termination, geo-steering, request coalescing, and the per-route cache policy. It does NOT cache any LLM-derived output (decision 5 R2 cap + W9 红线 2).
workers.name: g1n-api-gateway

observability.$comment_observability: Workers tail logs feed the same Grafana instance that scrapes the k8s pods. Sampling at 10% keeps the bill under the 1M-requests/month free tier.

vars.$comment_G1N_API_ORIGIN: Origin URL. Set per-environment: staging points at the staging k8s cluster, production at the prod cluster. The Workers route is stateless — every env var change is a redeploy, not a hot reload.

limits.$comment_cpu_ms: 50ms CPU budget per request — well under the action endpoint's 4s P95 target. The 4s budget lives at the origin (decision 5 R4); the Workers gateway adds at most 1ms of overhead.

bindings.name: G1N_STATIC_META
bindings.$comment_G1N_STATIC_META: KV holds the static scene metadata (immutable per case slug). Reads from KV are eventually consistent (< 60s globally), which is fine for scene metadata.

cache.$comment_cache: Per-route cache policy.  - artifacts (images / illustrations): 30 days, immutable  - audio (motifs / ambient / music): 90 days, immutable  - api: NO CACHE  The api rule is the W9 红线 2 enforcement: a stale LLM response would break decision 5 acceptance, so we set Cache-Control: no-store on every API route.

rules.name: artifacts-30d
rules.description: Illustrations + scene atmosphere images. Hash-named in the bundle so 30-day immutable cache is safe.

rules.name: audio-90d
rules.description: Audio motifs + ambient + music. Larger TTL because the files are bigger and rarely change.

rules.name: manifest-5m
rules.description: Vite build manifest. Short TTL so a new build rolls out within 5 minutes without purging.

rules.name: api-no-cache
rules.description: W9 红线 2 — every API route sets Cache-Control: no-store. The Workers gateway adds the header before forwarding to the origin so a misconfigured origin cannot leak a stale LLM response.

rules.name: health-bypass
rules.description: Health endpoint — bypass cache for accurate liveness signal.

purge.$comment_purge: The deploy pipeline calls this API after a successful build. We only purge the bundle paths (not the immutable assets) so the audio/image cache stays warm across deploys.
purge.$comment_on_demand_paths: Music files are bigger (5-15 MB each) and rarely updated; the deploy pipeline purges them explicitly when a new motif ships.

r2.$comment_r2: R2 holds the immutable asset bundle (images + audio) so the edge cache can serve them without going back to Pages. R2 egress is free within Cloudflare, so the W9 static-asset bill is essentially zero.

security.$comment_security: Defence-in-depth: WAF managed rules + bot score + rate limit + the page-rule headers above. The rate limit protects the W9 acceptance number (P95 < 4s) from a single bad actor saturating the cluster.

waf.$comment_managedRulesets: Cloudflare OWASP + Leaked Credentials rulesets. 'on' = block (default), not 'challenge', because the player base is not large enough to tolerate a CAPTCHA on every request.

botScore.$comment_challengeOn: Bot score 30 = 'likely automated'. Below 30 we serve a JS challenge to keep analytics scrapers out of the model_calls cost column.

rateLimit.$comment_rateLimit: 600 requests / minute / IP — the brief's '千人在线' (1000 concurrent) headroom: 1000 users × 0.6 actions/sec = 600 RPM, the edge of the limit. Above that the CF edge serves a managed challenge rather than forwarding to origin (so the origin P95 stays green).

transformRules.$comment_transformRules: Strip the X-Powered-By header (information disclosure); force the request through the Workers route (defence in depth — even if the k8s ingress is exposed, the Workers route is the public surface).

analytics.$comment_analytics: Cloudflare Analytics Engine + Logpush to the same Grafana Loki instance. The observability.py module's Prometheus metrics cover the origin; these rules cover the edge.

deploy.$comment_deploy: Reproducible deploy. The CI workflow wraps the wrangler command and reads the env vars from a CI secret store. The local developer uses `wrangler pages deploy ./client/dist --project-name g1n-client` and `wrangler deploy --config infra/cdn/workers/wrangler.toml`.

commands.cache_purge: wrangler pages deployment tail g1n-client | xargs -I {} curl -X POST 'https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/${CLOUDFLARE_ZONE_ID}/purge_cache' -H 'Authorization: Bearer ${CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN}' -d '{"files": ["/index.html", "/manifest.json"]}'

rollback.$comment_rollback: Pages keeps the last 50 deployments; rollback is a metadata flip, not a redeploy. Workers' rollback uses `wrangler rollback --message 'W9 hotfix'` which restores the previous script + bindings.

acceptanceChecklist.$comment_acceptanceChecklist: CI / pre-deploy gates. The deploy script fails if any of these return non-zero. Mirrors the W9 brief.
acceptanceChecklist.rate_limit_active: for i in $(seq 1 700); do curl -s -o /dev/null https://api.g1n.example.com/v1/catalog; done | tail -1 | grep -E '4(03|29|31)'
acceptanceChecklist.$comment_rate_limit_active: Above the 600 RPM threshold the response code flips to 4xx; the deployment fails the gate if every request returns 200 (the rule is not active).
